milestone_boundary: Contract/schema only: introduce versioned preview_manifest/1.0 JSON Schema and an offline, stdlib-only semantic validator for already-persisted dry-run preview bundles. No consumers (ledger, archive, agent-review), no execution, no module invocation, no scanner/network/subprocess, no findings/evidence, no scope/config writes.

references.name: OSV schema (schema_version literal + strict typed JSON)
references.reason: OSV's pattern of a required literal schema_version string plus closed, typed fields is a clean fit for a small, stable contract.
references.safety_notes: Adopt only the versioning/strictness style; do not import OSV semantics (advisory/vuln content) or any network fetch behavior.

references.name: CycloneDX metadata + component hashes (alg+content+path)
references.reason: The {algorithm, content, name, size} per-artifact shape maps well to binding persisted preview files to their digests.
references.safety_notes: Adapt only the descriptor shape; do not adopt full BOM scope, supplier/license fields, or any external resolution.

references.name: SARIF run/tool/result metadata
references.reason: Borrow the idea of a versioned tool/producer block (name + version + run identifier) bound to the artifacts.
references.safety_notes: Do not adopt SARIF's result/finding lifecycle, taxonomies, or any importer assumptions; this manifest is not a findings format.

references.name: DefectDojo importer/test metadata
references.reason: Tightly coupled to a service, engagement/test lifecycle, and finding ingestion; out of scope for an offline preview contract.
references.safety_notes: Avoid any field semantics that imply upload, triage state, or remote tracking.

references.name: Nuclei result/template metadata
references.reason: Conventions are driven by live scanning output and template fetching; embedding them risks normalizing target-touching defaults.
references.safety_notes: Do not import live-scan field semantics, severity heuristics, or template-source assumptions.

required_fields: run_id (matches existing safe-run-id regex used by module_runner)
artifacts (closed list of fixed allowlist entries)
artifacts[].relative_path (POSIX, anchored under preview/, no '..', no leading '/')
semantic_checks: schema_version is the exact literal preview_manifest/1.0
run_id matches the same safe-run-id regex enforced by P2-13 persistence
artifacts set equals the fixed allowlist exactly (no extras, no omissions); preview_manifest.json itself is NOT listed
each relative_path resolves inside runs/<run_id>/preview/ with no symlink, no '..', no absolute path, no Windows drive prefix
each artifact file exists, is a regular file (not symlink/dir/device), and stat size equals size_bytes
recomputed sha256 of each artifact equals the manifest sha256
bundle_consistency.status == "ok" and bundle_consistency.digest matches the digest field inside the persisted bundle_consistency.json
created_at is not in the future and not earlier than run.json's recorded run start
producer.name/version are consistent with the module_runner version that wrote the bundle
no field contains absolute paths, host names, secrets, target identifiers, or environment values
manifest JSON parses with no trailing data and no duplicate keys
recommended_contract.failure_mode: Fail-closed: raise a typed PreviewManifestValidationError with a short, redacted reason and the failing field path. Never mutate, repair, rename, move, or delete the bundle. Never write new files. Never execute modules or network calls. Validator is a pure read-only function returning (ok, errors[]) or raising on hard violations; caller decides logging.

implementation_constraints: Offline, data-only, stdlib only (json, re, hashlib, pathlib, datetime); no third-party schema libs, no network, no subprocess, no threads.
Validator must be a pure function over a path; it reads the bundle directory but never writes, deletes, or creates files.
No module execution, no scanner adapters, no findings/evidence/report code, no callbacks, no scope/config writes, no loot/secret access.
Reject symlinks anywhere in the bundle path chain (reuse the symlink-rejection helper used by P2-13).
All paths recorded and validated as POSIX-relative under runs/<run_id>/preview/; absolute paths are a hard error.
Schema is closed (additionalProperties: false at every object) so unknown fields fail loudly; forward compatibility is opt-in via a future preview_manifest/1.1.
Version negotiation is strict-equal for 1.0; no silent upgrades or downgrades.
Validator is not wired into module_runner write-path in P2-14; it is a standalone consumer-side check with its own CLI entrypoint that takes a bundle directory.
No new persistence surface, no new artifact types, no generic write/output hook; the artifact allowlist is hard-coded.
Errors must be redaction-safe (no echoing of raw file contents, target identifiers, or environment values).
tests_required: Golden valid fixture: full preview bundle passes validator.
Each required field missing -> rejection with field path.
additionalProperties: unknown top-level and nested field -> rejection.
Unsafe run_id (path chars, spaces, traversal, empty) -> rejection.
Artifact allowlist drift: extra artifact entry -> rejection; missing required artifact -> rejection; manifest listing itself -> rejection.
relative_path with '..', leading '/', backslash, or drive letter -> rejection.
Symlinked artifact, symlinked preview dir, symlinked run dir -> rejection.
sha256 mismatch (tampered file) -> rejection; size_bytes mismatch -> rejection.
bundle_consistency.status != "ok" -> rejection; bundle_consistency.digest mismatch vs file -> rejection.
created_at in the future or before run start -> rejection; non-UTC or non-RFC3339 timestamp -> rejection.
Duplicate JSON keys / trailing data / non-UTF-8 -> rejection with typed error.
Pure-function guarantee: validator run leaves filesystem mtimes and contents unchanged (assert via snapshot).
No-network / no-subprocess assertion: validator module imports do not reference socket/urllib/subprocess (static import test).
non_blocking_recommendations: Add a contracts/CHANGELOG.md entry policy so any future preview_manifest/1.x bump is reviewed as its own T3 change.
Reserve (but do not implement in P2-14) a top-level manifest_digest field plan for a later preview-ledger phase; document the deferral rather than adding it now.
Document a one-paragraph compatibility rule: 1.0 is strict-equal; additive optional fields require a new minor version and a new schema file.
Co-locate fixtures under tests/fixtures/preview_manifest/1.0/{valid,invalid_*}/ so future ledger/agent-review consumers can reuse them without re-deriving.
Keep the validator CLI output machine-readable (single-line JSON result) so future tooling can consume it without re-parsing prose.
safety_boundary: No scans, probes, callbacks, exploit/fuzz/brute-force, or target-touching behavior introduced.
No module execution, no subprocess, no network, no scanner adapters, no template fetching.
No changes to config/scope.txt, program scope, safe_target, dry-run gates, or live-mode toggles.
No findings, evidence, reports, notifications, or publishing surfaces added.
No loot/secret access; validator errors are redaction-safe.
Validator is read-only: never writes, repairs, deletes, or moves bundle files.
Manifest is a closed contract with a fixed artifact allowlist; it is not a generic file-write or output mechanism.
Stricter rule wins: if this contract conflicts with .hermes.md, scope, or the OSS Recon Gate, defer to the stricter constraint.
